lines: +// A small dot that flags the state of a record, task or interface element.
+// Add the live modifier to signal an active or live element, or the loading
+// modifier for an operation in progress.
+// The dot conveys its meaning through colour alone, so it needs an accessible
+// name to be perceivable. Give it role="img" and an aria-label describing the
+// state when it carries meaning; mark it aria-hidden="true" when it only
+// repeats an adjacent visible label and is purely decorative.
+    // Reserve the dot dimensions before the element is upgraded so the host
+    // does not collapse and shift the surrounding layout while the JavaScript
+    // Map a recurring domain concept to one of the base colours above, so the
+    // same concept looks identical wherever it appears in the backend.
+ * This file is part of the TYPO3 CMS project.
+ * It is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under
+ * the terms of the GNU General Public License, either version 2
+ * of the License, or any later version.
+ * For the full copyright and license information, please read the
+ * LICENSE.txt file that was distributed with this source code.
+ * The TYPO3 project - inspiring people to share!
+import { html, LitElement, nothing, type TemplateResult } from 'lit';
+// The first group are the base colours; the second group are semantic states
+// that map to one of those base colours in the stylesheet, so a recurring
+// domain concept looks identical wherever it appears in the backend. Keep the
+// semantic group small and curated rather than introducing new colours.
+// A semantic state may also imply a motion modifier, so a single state carries
+// both the colour and the activity animation of the concept it names.
+ * A small dot that flags the state of a record, task or interface element.
+ * Add the `live` attribute to signal that an element is active or live, or the
+ * `loading` attribute to signal an operation in progress. Provide a `label` to
+ * expose an accessible name (rendered as role="img" + aria-label) and a hover
+ * tooltip (via title); without it the indicator is purely decorative and hidden
+ * The `state` accepts either a base colour (success, danger, …) or a semantic
+ * state (active, online, running, disabled) that resolves to one of those
+ * colours and may carry its own motion, so `state="running"` is already
+ * animated while `state="active"` is a plain dot.
-    const adminRow = contentFrame.locator('#typo3-backend-user-list tr', { has: contentFrame.getByRole('link', { name: 'Klaus Admin online (admin)' }) });
+    const adminRow = contentFrame.locator('#typo3-backend-user-list tr', { has: contentFrame.getByRole('link', { name: 'Klaus Admin (admin)' }) });
await test.step('test the edit link', async () => {
-      await expect(page.frameLocator('iframe[name="list_frame"]').getByRole('row', { name: 'Open context menu Klaus Admin online (admin)', exact: true }).getByRole('link')).not.toBeVisible();
+      await expect(page.frameLocator('iframe[name="list_frame"]').getByRole('row', { name: 'online Open context menu Klaus Admin (admin)', exact: true }).getByRole('link')).not.toBeVisible();
